title: Integer Arithmetic Safety — Unchecked Blocks in Solidity 0.8+
summary: Audit all unchecked arithmetic blocks in Solidity 0.8+ contracts to verify that each block relies on a documented, tested invariant and that no user-controlled input reaches unchecked arithmetic without prior bounds validation.
preconditions: Contract contains at least one unchecked { } block. If no unchecked blocks exist, this KB is vacuously satisfied and need not be run.

cost_estimate.notes: Static tools locate unchecked blocks and flag user-input taint (items 1, 5). Invariant verification (item 2) and boundary testing (item 3) require manual test authoring and fuzzer setup.

checklist.item: Find all unchecked { } blocks in the contract. For each block, document in source comments or the audit report the specific invariant that makes overflow or underflow impossible at that site.
checklist.rationale: Solidity 0.8+ adds default overflow checks at gas cost. Developers use unchecked to save gas, but each use must have a documented justification — an absence of documentation is itself an audit finding, regardless of whether overflow is actually possible.

tooling.manual: Review AST or source for every unchecked block; flag any without a preceding invariant comment.

checklist.item: For each unchecked block, verify the documented invariant holds at every function entry point that can reach the block, under all possible call sequences.
checklist.rationale: An invariant that holds locally may be violated by a state change in another function. Verification must consider the full reachability graph, not just the local scope.

tooling.automated: echidna: write property that asserts invariant before each unchecked block; echidna-test Contract.sol --config echidna.yml
tooling.manual: Trace all call paths that reach each unchecked block; verify the invariant constraint is enforced at every entry.

checklist.item: Run boundary tests at type limits: uint256 max (2^256 - 1 = type(uint256).max), int256 min (-2^255 = type(int256).min) and max (2^255 - 1 = type(int256).max), and zero. Confirm no unchecked operation overflows or underflows at these boundaries.
checklist.rationale: Type-limit edges are the primary source of arithmetic vulnerabilities. Explicit boundary tests must be in the test suite for every unchecked block — fuzzing alone does not reliably hit these exact values.

tooling.automated: forge test --fuzz-runs 100000 (Foundry) — configure invariant tests with bound(x, 0, type(uint256).max)
tooling.manual: Write explicit Hardhat/Foundry unit tests for each unchecked block at type(uint256).max, type(int256).min, type(int256).max, and 0.

checklist.item: Check loop counters inside unchecked blocks for overflow. Verify that the maximum iteration count, given all reachable input values, cannot cause the counter to wrap around to zero.
checklist.rationale: Loop counter overflow in an unchecked block is a common gas-optimization bug that produces an infinite loop or incorrect termination, causing denial of service or silent logic failure at scale.

tooling.automated: slither --detect incorrect-equality (flags counter comparisons that could wrap); manual review for increment patterns
tooling.manual: For each unchecked loop counter: determine max array length or iteration bound from all callers; confirm counter type cannot wrap at that bound.

checklist.item: Confirm that no user-controlled input — calldata arguments, msg.value, or external return values — reaches unchecked arithmetic without prior validation that constrains the input within safe bounds.
checklist.rationale: Unchecked blocks are safe only when inputs are bounded by the contract's own logic. User-controlled inputs directly invalidate the invariant: an attacker can pass max uint256 and force an overflow or underflow.

tooling.automated: slither --detect controlled-array-length,taint — traces user-controlled values through the call graph
tooling.manual: For each unchecked block, identify all data sources and verify each is validated (bounded by require, checked arithmetic, or type constraint) before reaching the unchecked operation.

successConditions: Every unchecked block has a documented invariant in source comments or audit report
Invariant verified to hold at every reachable entry point under all call sequences
Boundary tests pass at type(uint256).max, type(int256).min, type(int256).max, and 0 for every unchecked operation
No loop counter overflow path found under maximum reachable iteration count
Zero user-controlled inputs reach unchecked arithmetic without upstream bounds validation
failureConditions: Any unchecked block lacks a documented invariant — HIGH
Invariant violated by a reachable state change in another function — HIGH
Any boundary test produces overflow or underflow — HIGH
Loop counter wraps to zero under a valid call sequence — HIGH
User-controlled input reaches unchecked arithmetic without validation — CRITICAL

inputs.name: contract_source
inputs.description: Solidity source files (.sol) for the contract under audit

inputs.name: test_suite
inputs.description: Existing test suite to extend with boundary and fuzz cases

outputs.name: overflow_clearance
outputs.description: True when all 5 checks pass with zero violations

outputs.name: unchecked_block_inventory
outputs.description: Map of all unchecked blocks with invariant status, boundary test coverage, and taint analysis result
